recipe.description: Can add ranch before taking off heat, but before baking
recipe.title: Buffalo chicken Dip

ingredient_lines.note: Optional, or jar of chopped "heart of
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Optional: 1 small can or jar of chopped "heart of artichoke"

steps.instruction: In a large sauce pan or even a dutch oven, heat your butter over medium heat until it's melted and mostly finished bubbling. Add in your artichoke hearts and onions, if using, and cook briefly (2 minutes or so). Then (or immediately, if no artichokes/onions), add the chicken, garlic powder, salt, pepper, and hot sauce. Mix together thoroughly and let the chicken absorb the sauce well.

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 1lb bag of sharp cheddar cheese, shredded

steps.instruction: If you feel like dirtying an extra dish, pour all this into a big mixing bowl. Otherwise, just turn off the heat and mix right in the pot. Anyway, mix in your softened cream cheese and about 1/3 of the cheddar cheese. Stir to incorporate well, then dump the mixture into a 9x13 baking dish and cover with the remaining cheese and the fried onions.
Bake @ 375F for about 20 minutes, or until it's all bubbly and delicious. Cover with foil, vent the foil with a fork or skewer to let the steam escape, and bring it on to school :-D
Serve with chips (yellow corn tortilla chips is my preference).
